title: ilex brachyphylla
intro: Ilex brachyphylla, also known as the Cuban holly, is a species of plant in the holly family (Aquifoliaceae), native to Cuba and the Isle of Youth. The Cuban holly has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves have been reported to possess various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a digestive aid. In herbal preparations, Ilex brachyphylla is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
